source_fact_summary: The public issue says a code-agent PR added skopeo inspect to skip sandbox image pulls when digests match. The linked PR reported pre-commit hooks passed and sandbox tests ran, but the target GitHub Actions runners did not have skopeo installed, so the new optimization could never activate there.
unsupported_claims: The runtime failure is an unreachable/no-op optimization path rather than a process crash; manual review should confirm it still fits the strict build-runtime mismatch boundary.
category_change_reason: No category change suggested: the source pairs successful static/test verification with a target runtime/deployment path where the new behavior was unavailable.
privacy_findings: Only public GitHub issue or PR links and short factual summaries are retained; no credentials, private files, raw logs, or local absolute paths were copied.
severity_reason: The feature's stated optimization could never fire in the target runner, but the fallback avoided a complete workflow crash.
missing_information: Manual review should confirm runner image contents, PR checks, and whether the no-skopeo smoke test is sufficient for formal reproduction.
scope_reason: The case involves Agent-authored project code that passed checks but failed to deliver its runtime behavior in the deployment environment. A local MVP can check dependency availability and branch reachability, though exact GitHub runner reproduction may need environment modeling.
notes: Codex review suggestion only: public-source accessibility and category/scope recommendations do not mark the candidate source_verified, approved, or reproduced. Manual confirmation remains required.
